response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile senior engineer with strong testing culture and high-performance systems exposure, but the role is fundamentally a hands-on C++ position for industrial automation. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a bachelor thesis project; his career since has centered on web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He shows no exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle practices, MQTT, industrial protocols, or automation-domain systems, which are core rather than advantageous requirements here.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic, plus an event-driven platform he founded
Strong mentoring and engineering-culture record: TDD, workshops, courses, and open-source test tooling
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, and it dates back a decade; no evidence of modern C++/STL depth
No industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443, or SDL exposure — a core, non-negotiable competency for this role
No MQTT, message-broker at industrial scale, or automation protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.); no Azure

key_factors.factor: Strong high-performance systems and mentoring track record
